Title: Cr3a, a candidate gene conferring fruit cracking resistance, was fine-mapped in an introgression line of Solanum lycopersicum L.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein kinase superfamily protein; IMPAIRED IN BABA-INDUCED STERILITY 1-like; Serine/Threonine protein kinase catalytic domain (STKc/S_TKc); CDKE/CDK12-like
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pericarp/outer epidermis; in cracking-resistant 121B expression is low at mature green and higher at breaker and red ripe; in cracking-susceptible 776A high at mature green; in transgenic 776B lower at red ripe than at mature green and breaker
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fruit cracking resistance-associated metabolite network including SAMe, coniferyl alcohol, coniferin, L-phenylalanine, coumarin, 13-HPODE, and 9-oxo-ODE
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to fruit pericarp structural integrity and resistance to longitudinal and ring cracking
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers tomato fruit cracking resistance; overexpression/introgression of Cr3a(650) alleviates cracking, while knockout causes cracking susceptibility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; qRT-PCR; transgenic overexpression in susceptible line 776A; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in resistant line 121B; whole-genome sequencing off-target check; phenotype observation; transcriptome-metabolome integration
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stages: mature green, breaker, red ripe
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; promoter cis-elements include MeJA-responsive TGACG motifs, MYBHv1-binding CCAAT boxes, MBS drought-responsive element, auxin-responsive TGA element, ABRE, LTR, and in Solyc03g115660 also TCA salicylic-acid-responsive elements
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cr3a was fine-mapped to a ~27.24-30 kb interval on chromosome 3. 2. Among two candidate genes, only Cr3a(650) was functionally validated as the fruit cracking resistance gene. 3. Knockout of Cr3a(650) converted resistant fruit to cracking-susceptible, whereas introduction into susceptible plants alleviated cracking. 4. Multi-omics linked Cr3a to shifts in phenylpropanoid, amino acid, cysteine/methionine, and linoleic acid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding fresh-market tomatoes with improved fruit cracking resistance through marker-assisted introgression, transgenic complementation, or genome editing
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although Cr3a influences nutrient-related metabolites, the primary trait studied is fruit cracking resistance rather than nutritional quality. Cr3a(660) was tested but excluded as causal because overexpression/knockout did not alter cracking phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ethylene biosynthesis and related ripening/stress processes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered SAMe level in Cr3a knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp between 121B and 121A at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MetK was identified as an upstream DEG in the cysteine and methionine metabolism pathway. 2. Its expression change was associated with altered SAMe abundance in Cr3a knockout fruit. 3. It is part of the Cr3a-linked metabolic network.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as part of downstream network rather than directly functionally validated by single-gene manipulation in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cysteine and methionine metabolism / ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosylmethionine into ACC, precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Consumes pathway metabolite and channels flux to ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Links SAM metabolism to ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression change associated with reduced SAMe in cracking-susceptible knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in ethylene biosynthesis, but not specifically established here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS1_2_6 was identified as a downstream DEG linked to SAMe changes. 2. It connects the Cr3a-associated network to ethylene biosynthesis. 3. It was included among 13 key genes from multi-omics integration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Network gene only; no direct transgenic validation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces cinnamyl aldehydes to cinnamyl alcohols including coniferyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignin precursor contributing to cell wall structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered coniferyl alcohol/coniferin levels in Cr3a network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAD was identified in the phenylpropanoid pathway linked to coniferyl alcohol/coniferin changes. 2. It was included in the Cr3a-associated cracking resistance network. 3. Lignin precursor metabolism was implicated in pericarp integrity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying lignin-related cracking resistance, with caution due to possible russeting/lignification effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess lignification may affect fruit surface quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct single-gene functional assay in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates caffeic acid derivatives in lignin precursor biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool toward coniferyl alcohol/coniferin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lignin precursor formation and structural integrity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with coniferyl alcohol/coniferin variation in Cr3a-related network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT was one of the key DEGs associated with phenylpropanoid metabolite shifts. 2. It was part of the Cr3a-linked lignin-related network. 3. The pathway was implicated in fruit cracking resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target in combination with wall-strength genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter lignin composition and fruit surface properties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional validation in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols during lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Consumes precursor and supports structural polymer deposition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with coniferyl alcohol/coniferin turnover in the Cr3a network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. POD was identified as a downstream DEG linked to coniferyl alcohol/coniferin metabolism. 2. It supports the lignin/cell wall branch implicated in cracking resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential support gene for cell-wall reinforcement breeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered oxidative polymerization may affect texture or browning-related properties

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates coniferyl alcohol to produce coniferin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with decreased coniferin in cracking-susceptible knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at mature green stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT72E was linked to coniferin accumulation changes in the Cr3a network. 2. It supports the phenylpropanoid branch associated with fruit cracking resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to modulate monolignol storage and wall-related traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct validation beyond omics association.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of prephenate in the phenylalanine biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of phenylpropanoids and lignin-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered L-phenylalanine level in Cr3a knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pheA2 was identified as an upstream DEG associated with L-phenylalanine changes. 2. It is part of the Cr3a-related amino acid/phenylpropanoid network.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter aromatic amino acid flux broadly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains mixed annotation wording around prephenate/4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate; retained cautiously as reported network gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aromatic amino acid precursor for phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with elevated L-phenylalanine in Cr3a knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADT was one of the upstream DEGs associated with L-phenylalanine change. 2. It participates in the Cr3a-linked red-ripe-stage aromatic amino acid network.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on aromatic amino acid metabolism possible

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls entry into lignin and secondary metabolite biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with L-phenylalanine and downstream phenylpropanoid changes in Cr3a network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL was identified as a key downstream DEG in the L-phenylalanine/phenylpropanoid network. 2. Its flux-control role supports involvement of lignin-related metabolism in cracking resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for modulating phenylpropanoid flux, but should be balanced against effects on flavor and defense compounds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly affect secondary metabolism, defense, and quality traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Network inference only in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: trans-Cinnamic acid or related cinnamate products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes ammonia from phenylalanine/tyrosine to initiate phenylpropanoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with altered aromatic amino acid utilization in Cr3a-related network
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTAL was included among the 13 key genes connected to Cr3a. 2. It links L-phenylalanine dynamics to phenylpropanoid metabolism at the red ripe stage.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could shift aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid balance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes beta-glucosides and is associated with coumarin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary metabolite associated with phenylpropanoid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation associated with increased coumarin in knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. beta-G was linked to coumarin accumulation changes in the Cr3a network. 2. It was included among the 13 key genes identified by integrated omics.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association only; exact coumarin step not fully resolved in paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Peroxidizes linoleic acid to form 13-hydroperoxy/octadecadienoic acid-related oxylipins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fatty acid oxidation and pericarp chemical composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation associated with reduced 13-HPODE in cracking-susceptible knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX2S was linked to 13-HPODE reduction in knockout fruit. 2. It is part of the Cr3a-associated linoleic acid metabolism branch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning pericarp oxylipin status in cracking resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect flavor volatiles, defense, and stress signaling

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 9S-lipoxygenation of linoleic acid to produce 9-oxylipin precursors leading to 9-oxo-ODE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor toward target metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fatty acid oxidation and pericarp composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation associated with reduced 9-oxo-ODE in cracking-susceptible knockout fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in pericarp at red ripe stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX1_5 was identified as an upstream DEG associated with 9-oxo-ODE. 2. It forms part of the Cr3a-linked linoleic acid metabolism network.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/target for pericarp oxidative metabolism traits
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on volatile profiles and defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional assay in this paper.
